req.title: System-Admin views leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-016 mandates that authenticated system-admins view leave-requests with mandatory field validation and audit logging. The test suite applies EP (equivalence classes: valid admin, invalid roles), BVA (boundary IDs, empty/max-length fields), NEG (missing auth, forbidden roles), STC (authenticated→authorized→viewed→audit), and DT (combinations of auth state, role, and module enablement). Three minimum test cases cover happy path (positive), authentication failure (negative), and role-boundary (boundary).

tests.name: test_admin_views_valid_leave_request_success
preconditions: system-admin authenticated with valid JWT token
leave-request with ID 'LR-001' exists in database
expected: response body contains leave_request entity with all mandatory fields populated
audit record exists with action='VIEW', actor=admin_user_id, entity_id=LR-001, timestamp recent
trace: R-WEB-016: authenticated system-admin shall be able to view
R-WEB-016: expected outcomes - leave-request is viewed; audit record written; response 200
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: EP - valid admin in valid state
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_admin_views_valid_leave_request_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    admin_token = <fixture_admin_token>
    leave_request_id = 'LR-001'
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = response.json()
    assert data['entity_id'] == leave_request_id
    assert 'employee_id' in data
    assert 'start_date' in data
    assert 'end_date' in data
    
    audit_response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/audit-logs',
        headers=headers,
        params={'entity_id': leave_request_id, 'action': 'VIEW'}
    )
    assert audit_response.status_code == 200
    audit_records = audit_response.json()
    assert len(audit_records) > 0
    latest = audit_records[-1]
    assert latest['action'] == 'VIEW'
    assert (datetime.fromisoformat(latest['timestamp']) > 
            datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(seconds=5))

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_leave_request
expected: response body contains error message mentioning authentication
no audit record created for this request
trace: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: NEG - missing required authentication credential
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_leave_request():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    leave_request_id = 'LR-002'
    
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}'
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data
    assert 'auth' in data.get('error', '').lower() or \
           'auth' in data.get('message', '').lower()

tests.name: test_user_with_forbidden_role_cannot_view_leave_request
preconditions: user authenticated with valid JWT token
expected: response body contains error indicating insufficient permissions
no audit record created for this unauthorized attempt
trace: R-WEB-016: Error pathways - 403 if role forbidden
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: BVA - role boundary (admin vs non-admin)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_user_with_forbidden_role_cannot_view_leave_request():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    employee_token = <fixture_employee_token>
    leave_request_id = 'LR-003'
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {employee_token}'}
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 403
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data
    assert 'permission' in data.get('error', '').lower() or \
           'forbidden' in data.get('message', '').lower()
    assert 'entity_id' not in data

tests.name: test_admin_views_leave_request_with_maximum_length_fields
preconditions: system-admin authenticated with valid JWT token
leave-request 'LR-MAX' exists with all text fields at maximum allowed length
actions: validate response contains all max-length fields intact
expected: response body contains leave_request with all fields intact (no truncation)
all mandatory fields present and valid
trace: R-WEB-016: Mandatory fields must be validated
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: BVA - boundary value (max field lengths)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_admin_views_leave_request_with_maximum_length_fields():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    admin_token = <fixture_admin_token>
    leave_request_id = 'LR-MAX'
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = response.json()
    assert len(data['reason']) <= 2000
    assert len(data['employee_id']) <= 50
    assert data['reason'] == data['reason'].strip()
    assert all(key in data for key in 
               ['entity_id', 'employee_id', 'start_date', 'end_date'])

tests.name: test_admin_views_nonexistent_leave_request_returns_404
preconditions: system-admin authenticated with valid JWT token
leave-request 'LR-NONEXIST' does not exist in database
trace: R-WEB-016: Error handling - resource not found
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_admin_views_nonexistent_leave_request_returns_404():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    admin_token = <fixture_admin_token>
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/leave-requests/LR-NONEXIST',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 404
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data

tests.name: test_admin_views_leave_request_with_expired_token
preconditions: user holds an expired JWT token (exp < now)
token was valid for admin role previously
actions: GET /leave-requests/LR-004 with expired token in Authorization header
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_admin_views_leave_request_with_expired_token():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    expired_token = <fixture_expired_admin_token>
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {expired_token}'}
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/leave-requests/LR-004',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401
    data = response.json()
    assert 'expired' in data.get('error', '').lower() or \
           'invalid' in data.get('message', '').lower()
